title: Ballard Power Q4 2025 Earnings Call Summary
text: Ballard Power delivered strong Q4 2025 results with revenue of approximately $34 million (up 37% YoY) and full-year revenue exceeding $99 million (up 43% YoY). The company achieved a 17% gross margin in Q4 and 5% for the full year, representing significant improvements from negative margins in prior periods. Cash operating costs in Q4 reduced by 41% compared to the prior year, demonstrating substantial structural improvements. Engine shipments approached 800 units delivering over 75 megawatts, a 38% increase in megawatts shipped. The company secured its largest marine contract ever at 6.4 megawatts from ECAP Marine and Samskip, plus a 50-megawatt agreement with New Flyer covering 500 engines. Management noted that the margin profile of the business is strengthening and is foundational for profitability goals. CEO Marty Neese outlined five growth drivers: improved commercial terms, product cost reductions, enhanced fleet services, expanded market reach, and innovative business models. The FCmove SC platform achieved a 40% reduction in total part count while improving performance and durability. Operating expenses are projected between $65-75 million for 2026, with capital expenditures of $5-10 million. Management guided to a 40/60 first-half/second-half revenue split and emphasized disciplined spending and margin expansion priorities.
